item.title: White House Uses GTA 6 Meme To Distract From Reflecting Pool Turning The Color Of Mountain Dew
item.content:encoded:
Today, the official White House account on Twitter decided to post the administration’s own ugly version of the recently revealed Grand Theft Auto 6 cover art featuring U.S. President Donald Trump with his slogan, “Make America Great Again,” rendered in the classic GTA font.

Team Trump no doubt hopes this distracts everyone online from how awful the Reflecting Pool looks as it turns green with algae and the President’s recent paint job reportedly peels off less than a month after it was “fixed.” Whoops. Did I mention the Reflecting Pool looks like it’s filled with Ecto-Cooler juice right now? Dang. Sorry, Mr. President. Won’t happen again!

Anyway, on June 18, shortly after Rockstar Games revealed the official cover art for Grand Theft Auto 6, the White House Twitter account posted an image, likely created with the help of generative AI, spoofing the iconic cover design of GTA games and using the pink and purple hues seen in the real GTA 6 artwork. The fake cover features four different images of Trump. The White House posted the image and added: “We really saved America before GTA 6.” Trump’s goons fucked up the placement of the helicopter, too. 

We really saved America before GTA 6 pic.twitter.com/W0ZRsLsVCj

— The White House (@WhiteHouse) June 18, 2026

The replies are a cesspool of racism and total nonsense that only people who spend hours consuming conservative, right-wing slop will even fully understand. I’d advise not even scrolling down.

Kotaku has contacted Rockstar Games about the image, but didn’t hear back before publication.

In the replies to that post, yes, I didn’t take my own advice; I even spotted someone joking about the Reflecting Pool in DC, which currently is filled with ugly green algae that makes it look like the world’s biggest pool of Mountain Dew. But don’t drink it! Officials have been pouring hydrogen peroxide, bottles of the stuff, into the pool in an attempt to kill off the algae. And that algae likely formed in part due to the new blue paint applied to the bottom of the pool. That blue paint is now allegedly flaking and peeling off the bottom, less than two weeks after being painted.

Oh dang, I wasn’t going to talk about that. Sorry, sir. Your video game-themed distractions on social media, like you posting Persona shit and Call of Duty memes, are totally winning over young voters who aren’t at all concerned about the state of the world, their future in it, and all the horrible shit you keep saying and doing. One more Halo joke or GTA meme will surely do the trick. Keep up the good work and don’t worry about how much greener the Reflecting Pool looks. Everything is fine.



https://kotaku.com/white-house-uses-gta-6-meme-to-distract-from-reflecting-pool-turning-the-color-of-mountain-dew-2000708154

item.title: ENC: European League of Legends Online Qualifier Brackets
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Nations Cup (ENC) online League of Legends qualifier brackets for both EU East and EU West have been revealed.

The Esports Foundation confirmed the 16 invited LoL ENC teams and rosters that topped the charts for the highest performance scores.

Now, another 14 slots need to be decided by online regional qualifiers, with two expected to qualify from both EU qualifiers.

Here is everything you need to know about the ENC LoL online qualifiers for European teams.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When Are the ENC LoL European Qualifiers?

Both the Europe East and Europe West online qualifiers begin on Friday June 19.

They will also both end on June 21 with two teams from each (four in total) qualifying for the final tournament.

As for the ENC qualifier format, all matches will be best-of-ones except for the Lower Bracket and Upper Bracket finals, which will be best-of-threes.

  • Related: Esports Nations Cup Rocket League rosters

ENC LoL Europe East Qualifier Rosters

  • Albania: Diobello / Kr0n / Alvanai / Ralf / iLevi
  • Bulgaria: Archfiend / Spooky / FrostSize / Vzz / Infoneral
  • Croatia: Sacre / Mafro / Perkz / Jopa / ThomasFate
  • Estonia: Ruf / yOFT / Klaabu / Jabuticaba / Richu
  • Hungary: Vizicsacsi / bluerzor / Robenong / Endless / Passzi
  • Israel: Music / Chelouche / Tissue / Shogo / Balagan
  • Kosovo: Supreme light / Desserte / Paradox / al1nyyy / Arsick
  • Latvia: Onyoz / Qualzy / Hjustons / Afriibi / Mango
  • Lithuania: Eckas / Lyncas / Toffe / Yashiro / Parein
  • Moldova: Kpr / Owlonsky / Babuleh / LeX / Hokage
  • Montenegro: Talja / Pegaz / Nafkelah / Lixo / Sheka
  • North Macedonia: Danki / Fallen / Pesho / Axelent / Nano
  • Romania: Shelfmade / Frost / Ronaldo / Yakkey / whiteinn
  • Serbia: APP / Stefan / VeRu / Shy Carry / Panj
  • Slovakia: Stein / zeen / SeMike / Raining / AwerpiS
  • Slovenia: Kektz / Nighthawk / Foxie / Sentio / KroMAX
  • Ukraine: Maynter / Maul / Fogzy / Jamie / Efias

ENC LoL Europe East Qualifier Bracket and Format

The online qualifiers will feature a double-elimination bracket, lose in the Lower Bracket and you’re out.

As mentioned prior, all matches will be best-of-ones except for the two finals, which will be best-of-threes to decide who qualifies for this year’s Esports Nations Cup.

In Europe East, only two teams will play in the opening round having been ranked lower than the other teams.

Opening Matches

  • Upper Bracket Round 1
    • Moldova vs Estonia
  • Upper Bracket Round 2
    • Serbia vs Moldova/Estonia
    • Romania vs Latvia
    • Ukraine vs Slovenia
    • Lithuania vs Kosovo
    • Croatia vs Bulgaria
    • Albania vs North Macedonia
    • Hungary vs Israel
    • Slovakia vs Montenegro

ENC LoL Europe West Rosters

  • Austria: Vertigo / Konkai / UniqueCORN / DizL / seaz
  • Belgium: Bwipo / Isma / Nisqy / Évangelyne / Targamas
  • Cyprus: Totyz / Limbo / Ivok / Zebron / SP1Nx
  • Finland: Nille / dibu / Simpli / Lari / Sammey
  • Germany: Irrelevant / Habubu / Reeker / Keduii / Tockimo
  • Great Britain: Shikari / White / BZ / Deadly / kaSing
  • Iceland: Baunadós / Icelandic Hero / Sigginn / Meoki / Kristut
  • Ireland: Allorim / Hybradge / BB Muffin / jinjo / Markgaffo
  • Italy: Gabbo / Goliah / Vigil / Ryuk / Click
  • Malta: Gruntless / Jar / Drakur / Hido / Awaken
  • Netherlands: Dan Dan / Markoon / Febiven / Furuy / Seal
  • Norway: Smurfegutten / DenSygeKamel69 / LIDER / Nomi / Nash
  • Portugal: PapiTeero / Time / Baca / FlickeR / Obstinatus
  • Switzerland: Pride / MoTo / Poro / Yen / Lucky

ENC LoL Europe West Qualifier Bracket and Format

While Europe West has the same format as the East qualifier, the bracket is slightly different.

Germany and Italy were higher ranked than the other teams, so received a bye into Upper Bracket Round 2.

Meanwhile, the rest of the teams will play in Upper Bracket Round 1.

Opening Matches

  • Ireland vs Norway
    • Winner faces Germany
  • Portugal vs Iceland
  • Belgium vs Malta
  • Great Britain vs Austria
    • Winner faces Italy
  • Netherlands vs Cyprus
  • Finland vs Switzerland

Which four teams do you expect to qualify? Let us now on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, the LCS Spring Split 2026 recorded its lowest-ever viewership despite Jynxzi co-streaming the final.

item.title: Ghost of Yotei is Reportedly Carrying the Load for First-Party PlayStation Games in 2026 as it nears an Estimated 5M Copies Sold
item.content:encoded:
Last year's Ghost of Yotei is currently this year's best-selling first-party PlayStation Studios title, according to a new report from Alinea Analytics. The latest from Sucker Punch is closing in on an estimated 5 million copies sold since launch, the report claims, and as we officially transition to the back-half of 2026, it's estimated 1.1 million copies sold within 2026 is carrying the load for PlayStation's first-party portfolio.

That's not the biggest shock, considering the only other first-party PlayStation releases so far this year are Marathon and Saros, both games that received heavy critical praise, but neither has been able to make a splash commercially. Marathon's woes have been well-documented, and just last month, Alinea Analytics claimed that Saros could "struggle to break even," despite thousands of Returnal fans showing up for the game at launch.

But if you told someone in January 2026 that Yotei would still be the game bringing in more revenue for PlayStation than any of its other recent releases, they might've been surprised to hear it then. Where PlayStation's portfolio finds itself now is closer to a worst-case scenario for the first half of the year, and very likely not where leadership wants to be.

While Saros and Marathon aren't pulling their weight, the game from PlayStation's first-party suite with a more consistent batting average is Gran Turismo 7, a game that arrived two years after the PS5 did in 2022. It's the latest entry in a franchise known for its games having a long tail, but that doesn't make its ability to keep that reputation alive any less impressive.

It's even sitting above MLB The Show 26, according to Alinea, for copies sold in 2026 so far. Outside of that top three, it's 2023's Marvel's Spider-Man 2 and Astro Bot to round out the top five, with Saros slotted in sixth place.

The report also claims that while Yotei is selling fewer copies than its predecessor, Ghost of Tsushima, it's bringing in more revenue as it maintains its higher price point for a longer period of time.

But even with Yotei continuing to bring in revenue and sell well, the clear takeaway for PlayStation is that it needs more from its first-party suite. Thankfully, Marvel's Wolverine is around the corner in September 2026, but with that month and the rest of the year as packed as it already is, Wolverine does not have a clear path to selling millions of copies right away.

It'll be interesting to see where it lands when September rolls around, and if it can at least win the month on the sales charts.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/ghost-of-yotei-nears-5m-copies-sold-carrying-the-load-playstation-first-party-2026/

item.title: Bloodborne Can Finally Run At 120 FPS On PS5, But Only By Using A Mod
item.content:encoded:
Bloodborne, the unforgettable FromSoftware-developed PlayStation 4-exclusive soulslike that publisher Sony has seemingly forgotten about, can finally run at 120 FPS on PlayStation hardware, but with one caveat: you’ll need a modded PlayStation 5 to pull it off.

For those who own relatively beefy PCs, running Bloodborne at 120 FPS has been possible for a while now through the use of the PlayStation 4 emulator ShadPS4. However, the dream for many has always been to play Bloodborne at something higher than 30 FPS on native hardware. Well, maybe that’s less of a dream and more of a necessity, considering that PCs aren’t exactly cheap to build these days.

Officially, running Bloodborne at anything higher than the 30 FPS it’s locked to isn’t possible on PlayStation hardware. Unofficially, however, running it at 60 FPS on a PlayStation 4 has been possible for several years through the use of jailbreaking. It would seem that 120 FPS is a bridge too far for the almost 13-year-old console, though, which is where the PlayStation 5’s backwards compatibility comes into play.

According to a report by Digital Foundry, an upcoming mod for Bloodborne by a modder simply known as Christina allows for a consistent 120 FPS on jailbroken PlayStation 5s. Through the use of a frame-rate unlock patch, Bloodborne can take advantage of the PS5’s 120Hz mode at 1080p resolution. Digital Foundry also states that it’s possible to increase Bloodborne’s “native rendering resolution to full 4K,” but this comes at the cost of being locked to 30 FPS.

Digital Foundry noted that while Christina’s Bloodborne mod isn’t available quite yet, they “understand that these mods will be released shortly.” But, again, I should reiterate that this isn’t possible if you don’t own a modded PlayStation 5 or an unpatched PS5 that’s running firmware from pre-circa 2022. Still, it’s interesting that it’s possible at all on PlayStation hardware, and, once again, raises the question as to why Sony hasn’t released 60 FPS support (let alone 120 FPS) for the title on PlayStation 5 hardware.



https://kotaku.com/bloodborne-ps5-ps4-30fps-60fps-120fps-mod-4k-sony-2000708118

item.title: 'A new industry with a new identity:' How Canadian devs are faring in 2026
item.content:encoded:
All images via XP GamingAll images via XP Gaming

Now in its fifth year, Toronto Canada's XP Game Summit–a sister conference to the long-running Montreal International Game Summit–offered a unique opportunity to take the temperature of a Canadian games industry that, as in other regional industries, has found itself battered by the winds of change. 

Despite doubling in size between 2013 and 2022, the industry has suffered recently due to what some have argued is an outsized emphasis on generous tax incentives slanted towards triple-A multinationals with little oversight or protection for workers. In recent years, this has meant the industry has seen seismic studio closures and layoffs, with Embracer group shutting down Onoma (once Square Enix Montreal) and Ubisoft closing Ubisoft Halifax shortly after the studio unionized (although the company claims there was no connection).

It's no surprise then that at the XP Summit, attendees were bearish on the future of triple-A development in Canada. What might surprise some is that despite this, the mood at XP Game Summit was optimistic: couched in the recognition that the global games industry has changed drastically due to the "pandemic hangover."

Related:Pocketpair lead on generative AI: Gamers don't want it

Jason Lepine, XP Summit's founder, feels that within five years the summit has already seen it all.

"We launched right before massive layoffs hit the industry," he said. "Now, though, it feels like we're moving past that phase. Three years ago it was difficult just to get investors to attend events; now we're seeing more contracts getting signed, more recruiting activity, and a general sense that momentum is coming back."

Not recovery, transformation

However, he was quick to emphasize that this change did not represent "recovery" but "transformation."

"It's important to say: this isn't a return to the old industry. It's a new industry with a new identity."

That identity? One that supports smaller teams able to move quickly to validate their own market potential.

Jason Della Rocca, co-founder of Excution Labs explained the new mindset required while giving his talk, "The New Timeline for Indie Success."

"The reality is that publishers and investors are not your client," he said, arguing against developers that retain a traditional focus on pitching to publishers. "Since [the COVID era] a lot of investors, a lot of publishers, are only making investments based on evidence. Deals these days are not done based only on that the team is cool and skilled and can deliver. Not only that the game feels good. But that you have wishlists, trailer views, a community."

Related:Vampire Survivors dev 'reviewing' Fortnite collab after Epic's AI announcement

Of course, he admitted the paradox at the heart of this advice: you need evidence to get funding but you need funding to generate that evidence.

The XP Summit sessions had a particular focus on this, with a general theme that Canada's new X factor was that—despite comparatively higher labor costs—smaller developers could rely on support systems such as the Canadian Media Fund to allow them to stretch their runway into a viable product before having to turn to more traditional forms of funding.

But this was not considered a silver bullet, with speakers such as Nordicity's Kristian Roberts arguing in conversation with Game Developer that in order to maintain competitiveness, organizations such as the CMF needed to more deeply consider what it chooses to fund.

"Historically, CMF funding has focused on making products, not necessarily making commercially viable products," he said. "That distinction matters. Post-2022, investment in games has dropped dramatically … If your support system isn't designed to encourage market-oriented or audience-oriented decision-making, then you're not necessarily generating sustainable success."

There are increasing signs that the CMF are more focused on commercial viability. Their new Commercial Third Party Investment Fund provides funds to projects that already have third party investment under the understanding that this represents proof of market viability. Developers we spoke to however noted the fund seemed paradoxical at a show where third party investment was not merely considered difficult to get but far less attractive than self-publishing.

Related:Don't Nod pursuing 'several levers' to secure financial future after Tencent decides against short-term investment

"The [funding] model's broken, right? It's broken," said Shum Sing, Managing Director of Agnito Capital during the "State of Game Funding" roundtable. "Investors don't want to take risks until you have demonstrable attraction, but at that stage, you're asking the question 'why do I need you?' I could go get some bank debt... I could work with some smaller marketing agencies. There are all types of options available for indie developers to sidestep investment."

From the developer perspective, there wasn't a sense that it was quite that easy. Everyone Game Developer spoke to agreed that securing any form of funding was harder than ever, and that survival—even for indies—could mean cuts.

It comes down to burn rate," Andrew Carvalho, co-founder of Laundry Bear argued. "The studios that have survived–including ours–are the ones that have stayed lean. We've had layoffs. We've had to reduce hours. We've made cuts wherever we've needed to."

Community and collaboration are keys to the future

While government funding was a factor, developers expressed that community and collaboration was a major reason for the Canadian games industry's continued survival.

As a co-developer, Carvalho stated that they had "more contracts with Canadian studios than we've had in the past four years," noting that independent Canadian developers that had success were redistributing their success to others, such as Black Tabby Games, who recently got into publishing after the success of Slay The Princess.

"While some teams have been hit really hard, the teams that survived are often trying to support others," he said. "It's not ideal, but it's definitely not as catastrophic as what we're seeing in parts of the U.S."

This collaborative view of the games industry is shared by Eileen Mary Holowka, at the show representing Baby Ghosts, a nonprofit created to support cooperative game developers.

"The old model of 'build a game, get a publisher, scale endlessly' just isn't reliable anymore," they said. "The giant studios are showing the limits of their own models. Ubisoft shutting down Halifax after unionization is obviously discouraging, but we're seeing successful indie studios helping fund other indies," they stated. "There's power in collective organizing."

Holowka also noted that the signs were encouraging for developers interested in operating as a cooperative, with the CMF now allowing cooperatives to apply for funding.

Such moves were viewed as part of a genuine shift from Canada's support system to reflect the new development landscape, and while the majority opinion was that there was more Canada could do to support its games industry, attendees didn't have a singular focus on access to funding.

"The easy answer is always 'more money'," said Carvalho, "Canada has the talent; what we're missing is better industry connectivity. We shouldn't always have to leave the country to build meaningful industry relationships."

Lepine agreed. "There's a real opportunity to strengthen the industry domestically by collaborating more closely with each other here at home." As for those famous tax credits? "Tax credits still absolutely have a role," said Roberts, who in particular pointed out New Zealand's tax credit system as a model to emulate. "But we can't rely on tax credits as the primary support mechanism forever."

"We need to think about the total ecosystem more cohesively–not just funding, but trade missions, startup support, mentorship, education, and how we prepare developers for a world where self-publishing, audience development, and small teams are becoming the norm."

About the Author

Blogger

Mathew Kumar is a graduate of Computer Games Technology at the University of Paisley, Scotland, and is now a freelance journalist in Toronto, Canada.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/-a-new-industry-with-a-new-identity-how-canadian-devs-are-faring-in-2026

item.title: NYT Strands #837 Hints and Answers for Today, June 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #837 for Thursday, June 18, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

Before getting into the answers of today’s puzzle, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android. 

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 18, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #837 are all words related to creatures found under the sea.

General Strands Hints for June 18

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: People usually eat the legs of this creature, potentially with some Old Bay Seasoning.
  • Hint 2: 1, 2, Red, Blue.
  • Hint 3: The villain on SpongeBob who is always trying to steal the Krabby Patty formula.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #837 for June 18, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 18, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CORN
  • EAGLE
  • PLAN
  • REEF
  • SPLASH

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #837 on June 18, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SHARK
  • URCHIN
  • PLANKTON
  • ALGAE
  • SEAWEED
  • FISH
  • CRAB
  • SPANAGRAM: CORALREEF

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #837?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 19 will be Strands Game #838

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Palworld lead on generative AI: Gamers don't want it
item.content:encoded:
Pocketpair communications director and publishing manager John Buckley affirmed the company, which is behind the popular Palworld, doesn't use generative AI because players don't want it.

Speaking to GamesRadar+, Buckley's response was short and to the point: "Gamers don't want it, and if the gamers don't want it, I guess that's it, right? Not much of a conversation to be had."

The statement comes amidst studios continuing to toot the genAI horn, despite the fact that it has garnered a largely negative reception from developers in recent years, with many arguing that it is having a corrosive effect on the industry.

Last year, Buckley spoke to us about the studio's anti-genAI stance in an interview, saying Pocketpair "doesn't believe in it." This was said in the context of the company being interested in helping to fund and publish games from other developers, so long as the tech isn't being used.

Related:'A new industry with a new identity:' How Canadian devs are faring in 2026

"People are going to say I'm lying, but these are just the hard facts. We don't believe in it. We're very upfront about it. If you're big on AI stuff or your game is Web3 or uses NFTs, there are lots of publishers out there [who'll talk to you], but we're not the right partner for that," Buckley said.

Speaking to GamesRadar+, Buckley elaborated further on those comments, tackling the public reception as well as Steam's mandated AI usage disclosure.

"I'm sure some people are doing it," he told the publication. "It's not really for us. I personally can't imagine it ever being too prominent. I'm sure there are some companies out there, some big companies, that are maybe using it to 'save time.' I don't know. I don't really think gen AI has much—I don't want to call it a bubble, but I don't know how much longer it's gonna keep up. Even Steam is pushing back a certain amount on it."

Buckley also makes a note of how some developers have started to clarify, unprompted, that their games are 100 percent human-made across emails and store pages.

"I don't know if we need to now start saying this is 100 percent human-made," Buckley added. "I think we should just all assume games are human-made unless said otherwise. I think it's a bit dystopian that it might end up like that. We'll have to put a Steam disclaimer, 'This game is made by humans.' That's kind of sad to think about."

When it comes to AI disclosure (or lack thereof), there have been plenty of recent examples. Owlcat Games confirmed the use of genAI to build its upcoming RPG, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn. Companies like Capcom have told investors that it intends to leverage the technology to "improve efficiency and productivity of game development."

Related:Vampire Survivors dev 'reviewing' Fortnite collab after Epic's AI announcement

Some developers claim that they will use the technology, but not to create assets. There have been several cases of games shipping with AI-generated copy or imagery—Crimson Desert developer Pearl Abyss recently apologized to players for failing to disclose the use of genAI during production. The South Korean studio explained it created some placeholder assets using "experimental AI generative tools." Some of those assets were subsequently found in the finished game.

Last year, Ubisoft and 11 Bit Studios issued similar apologies after placeholder assets produced using genAI were included in Anno 117: Pax Romana and The Alters.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/palworld-lead-on-genai-gamers-don-t-want-it

item.title: The interstellar dice-driven RPG Citizen Sleeper is free for the next week, so now you've got no excuse to not play it
item.content:encoded:
Hurrah! Today is a very fine Thursday, as one of the most tightly written sci-fi RPGs around is available to pick up for free for around a week. That'll be Citizen Sleeper, by the way, a dice-driven RPG that I'm sure all of you must already know and love, but for the selection of you that don't technically exist, I'm sure you'll be happy to be able to pick it up for the cost of a couple of clicks.

Specifically, it's free over at the Epic Games Store, so you'll need to do your monthly sign-in to the storefront for the exact purpose of picking up something cool that's currently free you swear you'll get round to eventually. You really should bump Citizen Sleeper up on your backlog list though. I'll even tell you why!

Inspired by tabletop games, Citizen Sleeper pits you as a sleeper, a digitised human consciousness that exists inside of a robot body. At the start of the game, you choose one of a selection of classes, each with their own particular sets of skills, buffs, and difficulties. Then, with no memories of your previous, flesh human life, you find yourself on a space station called the Eye, where you must do what you can to survive, all while trying to fend off a corporation that owns your mechanical body.

It's a tightly woven piece that navigates complex ideas around identity, chosen family, how capitalist systems may persist even in the far flung future, and a lot more beyond that. The character designs are top notch from start to finish, the music does a stellar job of establishing a sense of place in a game where you don't get to see much of one, and the fear of getting a bad roll is unparalleled. It's been a while since I last played Citizen Sleeper, but it's always somewhere in the back of my mind. Worth picking up, especially for free! You have until June 25th to do so, so don't put it off if it's been on your list.

Lead developer on the games Gareth Damian Martin just announced their next game too, Signet City, a first-person fungalpunk RPG. You can check it Julian's great conversation with Martin right here.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/the-interstellar-dice-driven-rpg-citizen-sleeper-is-free-for-the-next-week-so-now-youve-got-no-excuse-to-not-play-it

item.title: Marathon Players Earned 77 Days Worth Of Loot In Just One Week: ‘I Think This Season Is Cooked’
item.content:encoded:
We’re just over two weeks into Marathon season 2 and, well, things could be better. Bungie’s sci-fi extraction shooter has many new wrinkles for players to navigate, but also some new issues. The big one is a bloated loot economy. The studio confirmed that players had obtained the same amount of loot after the first week of season 2 as they had by week 11 in the first season. A mysterious bug is also causing more high-rarity items to spawn than planned. “I think this season is cooked,” wrote one streamer in response. “Just leave everything bloated. Worry about making mid-season content fun and having more balance next season with progression.”

A new update went live on June 16 that made some balance tweaks to Marathon. The patch added weekly rewards but slightly reduced the rate of Prestige drops and the overall loot quality, and nerfed some of the gear that enemies were spawning with. The XP earned from certain gear discovered in the Cradle, season 2’s new point of interest on Night Marsh, was also reduced. But these are just some initial changes. It’s clear Bungie feels there are deeper problems plaguing the game’s loot economy.

It wrote on X this week:

  • There’s an issue causing higher rarity loot to be more prevalent throughout the game than we see internally. This isn’t something we’ve seen in our internal playtests, and we’re still unravelling the source.
  • There were various bugs and other issues we’ve fixed in recent updates, such as guaranteed gold drops in places that shouldn’t have them.
  • While not the main cause, the influx of Sponsored Kits at the start of the season bolstered progression speeds.
  • While also not the main cause, the Sponsored Survival grind combined with an issue with the Complex Control chest also contributed to bloated Vaults.
  • The combination of these things has led to increased progression across the board, including with the Cradle.

This lines up with how some players have been feeling this season. As the game’s player pool has been winnowed down, the hardcore fans who remain are more likely to be investing lots of time in the daily grind. The result has been vaults full of blue and purple gear by the end of week two. Streamers especially are finding themselves so flush with top-end loot it sort of defeats the whole purpose of playing.

But now that it’s clear progression has been way too fast this season, players are divided on how to deal with it. Those who can’t play as much as their sweaty counterparts now feel like they’ll be left behind and at a disadvantage for the rest of the summer. Some even think Bungie should just roll with it and wait until season 3 for a clean break. “Maybe they should just call in an emergency wipe and start over lol,” joked one player on the subreddit.

It’s the latest challenge facing a game that can’t seem to catch a break this season, which launched to severe server issues and some confusion over the discounted Deluxe Edition that ended with some players getting it for free. With the game’s extended free week in the rearview mirror, the concurrent player counts on Steam are back to their pre-season-2 trough. Hopefully, most of those lost players just all temporarily switched over to Destiny 2 for its final farewell.



https://kotaku.com/marathon-players-earned-77-days-worth-of-loot-in-just-one-week-i-think-this-season-is-cooked-2000708129

item.title: Holy Crap, Vice City Looks Amazing In This New GTA 6 Clip
item.content:encoded:
I expected Grand Theft Auto 6 to be a big game. And every screenshot and trailer we’ve seen of it has only reinforced that idea. And yet, when I scrolled the newly updated official GTA 6 website today, I was stunned by the sheer scope of the world of Vice City. And I wasn’t alone.

On June 18, Rockstar Games revealed the official cover art for GTA 6 (don’t worry, it has a helicopter on it) and also announced that pre-orders for the highly anticipated game would go live on June 25 on console storefronts and at “select retailers.” It also quietly updated the official Grand Theft Auto 6 website a bit, adding the game’s cover artwork. And more excitingly, Rockstar added a new eight-second clip of Vice City to the site as you scroll down.

Thankfully, if you dig into the site’s code, you can pluck out the full, high-res video to gawk at. This is what longtime GTA leaker and dataminer TexFunz2 did, as you can see below:

A new look at Vice Cityhttps://t.co/6RyGImp23Q#GTAVI pic.twitter.com/4WxuckIHUF

— Tex2 (@TexFunz2) June 18, 2026

And just…wow. I have a full-resolution version of the video on my PC, and I can’t stop looking at it. The city looks incredibly detailed and dense. You can look far off into the distance and see lights and buildings that look miles and miles away. It’s truly impressive. And keep in mind that Rockstar Games has historically used in-engine footage and real gameplay for all of their trailers. So this isn’t some pre-rendered nonsense, but instead a genuine look at what Vice City will look like in GTA 6. Wowza.

Reactions online were mostly positive, with some trolls claiming it looked bad, which is just sort of a wild thing to post online, but okay.

“The attention to detail in this city is insane,” posted one user about the new clip.

“What’s so beautiful about this is how the lights look at night time especially on the street. I just came back from Miami and the accuracy is literally insane,” said another person.

Another user shared a comparison of Vice City at night as it looked in the PS2 game compared to GTA 6‘s version of the city. It really sums up how far things have come in the 20+ years since Rockstar developed that game.

pic.twitter.com/jcIILijL4h

— BigBossSnakeWolfCorn (@BBSnakeCorn) June 18, 2026

Grand Theft Auto 6 launches on November 19 on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S. You can pre-order the game starting on June 25. I don’t expect any delays this time. 



https://kotaku.com/holy-crap-vice-city-looks-amazing-in-this-new-gta-6-clip-2000708110

item.title: Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports Release on PS5—All Differences Between Old and New Versions
item.content:encoded:
Now that the Black Ops 1 and 2 ports are public knowledge, it’s time to find out when we can look forward to both games coming out on PS5 and when you can download them.

Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 were rated in Korea, which was cool. We all wondered when they would be announced, and eventually they were. The countdown is on until the release date for BLOPS 1 and BLOPS 2 again. What a time to be alive.

Before I get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When are Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports Coming Out?

Treyarch has confirmed that the Black Ops 1 and 2 ports will be released in July 2026.

I wish I could narrow it down, unfortunately. Activision is remaining tight-lipped on the exact release dates for now, but unless there’s a delay, we are mere weeks away from both games getting another release, this time in 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports Platforms

Keep checking back with Insider Gaming, as we’ll eventually be able to bring you a concrete release date or two.

Black Ops 1 and 2 Port Differences Compared to Originals

From what dataminers have been able to gather, the PS4 and PS5 ports might include new 120 FPS graphical settings, new servers for online play, and new Black Ops 1 and 2 PlayStation Trophies.

RemasterRadar has provided a lot of information about the Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports so far.

  • More Insider Gaming: Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

They believe that while these are not remasters, both games will include new features and improvements over the original games.

  • ‘New languages.
  • PS5-enhanced flags (real improvements, likely a 120fps mode given the Warzone precedent).
  • PS4 Pro flag on BO2 (documented Sony NEO flag).
  • Modern PS4/PS5 PSN infra, NOT the dead PS3 servers.
  • Fresh build (Everyone starts from scratch in multiplayer).
  • New trophies.’

We’ll have to wait for the full launch to see if all of these alleged changes and updates are real, and if so, they are good reasons to buy each game again for the 37th time.

Let’s turn back the clock and watch the original and classic Black Ops 2 reveal trailer.

Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re buying Black Ops 1 or 2 on PS5.

item.title: Dead by Daylight Goes FREE on XBOX For All Users This Weekend
item.content:encoded:
XBOX has announced a wave of games going free to play this weekend, including some that are gated by Game Pass, and others that are fully accessible to all XBOX users. Fortunately, the one game with no access restrictions is arguably the best, as it’s absolutely booming right now thanks to a massive tenth-anniversary update.

That’s right, Dead by Daylight is free to play from today until Monday, June 22, for all XBOX users, regardless of whether they have Game Pass or not.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

XBOX Free Play Days Games Revealed

This weekend, four games are free to play on XBOX, but some will require you to have an active XBOX Game Pass subscription before you can access them.

First up is PGA Tour 2K25, which is great if you want to avoid enjoying the sunshine outside to instead enjoy the nice weather (perhaps) on the links. Not only is the Free Play Days promotion live to give you a taste of this game, but you can also get a hefty discount if you decide you want to hang onto it forever.

Next, you can get Two Point Museum, one of the most highly rated games ever made and one of the shining gems of Two Point Studios. In this game, you’ll become a gifted curator, designing and running a range of museums to accommodate eager and excited guests.

  • RELATED: Our Two Point Museum Review

Third, there’s Assetto Corsa, one of the most beloved and faithful racing simulation titles on the market, giving games like Forza a run for their money. It’s a highly detailed game with masterfully created physics and a massive library of the most attractive cars you’ll ever see.

These games are all accessible to those with XBOX Game Pass at the Ultimate, Premium, or Essential tiers.

But for every XBOX user, there’s another game in store – Dead by Daylight.

DBD recently celebrated its tenth anniversary, introducing a monumental update and bringing arguably the most iconic horror character of all time into the fold – Jason Voorhees. Of course, access to Jason isn’t free, but you can get a sampling of this legendary horror title and see if you want to invest later.

Are you going to play any of these games this weekend? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that XBOX is proudly displaying games’ exclusive status and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

item.title: Crime Boss: Rockay City Hits 1M Copies Sold and Celebrates Two-Year Steam Anniversary with a Massive 2.0 Update
item.content:encoded:
When Crime Boss: Rockay City launched in 2023, first on PC via the Epic Games Store and later on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S consoles, it seemed like the latest celebrity-backed dud. It was critically panned on launch, and despite its Hollywood star power getting a lot of eyes on it, lacking gameplay and several severe technical issues earned it low scores across the board. But that was three years ago. Now, it's reached over 1 million copies sold and is celebrating its two-year Steam anniversary with a major free 2.0 update.

Wccftech's Alessio Palumbo called Rockay City a "half-baked, rushed heist" in his initial impressions piece when the game launched, acknowledging that it was not worth checking out at the time, but could improve with future support and updates. Developer INGAME Studios had committed to providing that support, and it has never wavered over the past three years.

A year after its EGS release, it arrived on Steam, providing several key technical and content updates along the way. Today, it debuted its biggest update yet with Update 21, introducing the long-awaited story mode on all platforms and the full release of the ModKit for PC players, so they can create their own heists.

"We've never stopped believing in Crime Boss: Rockay City and the unique experience it can offer players," said INGAME Studios chief executive officer, Jarek Kolar, in a press release. "Update 21 represents everything we've learned over the past three years. It's our biggest step yet toward making the game more accessible, more creative, and more enjoyable for every type of player."

In no way did Crime Boss: Rockay City have the start that 505 Games or INGAME Studios wanted. No one aims for a 52 on Metacritic, or for their game to release in a state that our own Alessio Palumbo called "an early access release, and a middling one at that."

But it has had the upwards arc that so many strive for. It's never been a smash hit, of course, but it has consistently improved and sold more copies as INGAME Studios never stopped trying to make the game better. It's the kind of arc that a game like last year's MindsEye, for example, would dream of.

It's been an interesting game to follow, and it'll be even more interesting to see if the game that it has grown into over these last few years can suddenly pick up more momentum.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/crime-boss-rockay-city-1m-copies-sold-2-0-update/

item.title: Are Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports Coming to Xbox Series X|S, PC, Switch, or PS5? All Platforms
item.content:encoded:
Activision is delivering new ports of the first two Call of Duty Black Ops games. Whether you’re on PlayStation, Xbox, or PC, it’s important to know where and how you can enjoy the Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports.

The Black Ops 1 and 2 port gossip was more than just a rumor. It was revealed in June 2026 that Black Ops and Black Ops 2 ports were coming. On the other hand, fans aren’t as clear on WHERE they’re coming to, which I’m clearing up for you today.

Before I get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Can You Play Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports on PS5 or PS4?

Yes, we know for sure that the Black Ops 1 and 2 ports will be available to buy on PlayStation 4 and PS5 once they come out in July.

Treyarch‘s initial announcement made it clear that ‘Black Ops and Black Ops 2 are being ported to PlayStation.’ Obviously, by putting PlayStation in a broader sense, this means you should be able to buy them on both PS4 and PS5.

  • More Insider Gaming: Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

Last-gen support is ending with Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, but as BLOPS 1 and 2 are ports instead of modern-day remasters, the tech is still there to ensure they run great on PlayStation 4.

Can You Play Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports on Xbox Series X|S or Xbox One?

Right now, it doesn’t seem like you can play the new Black Ops 1 and 2 ports on either the Xbox One or the superior Xbox Series X|S devices.

Curiously, neither Treyarch nor Activision has mentioned anything about the Black Ops 1 and 2 ports coming to Xbox. PlayStation players get to benefit from this, even though Sony doesn’t have an exclusivity agreement with Call of Duty.

Go figure.

Can You Play Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports on PC?

The same fate falls on PC players as it does on the Xbox; you can’t play the new Black Ops 1 and 2 ports on a computer.

The only way you can play older Black Ops games on PC is by buying and playing the originals.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Pre-Order GTA 6—Grand Theft Auto 6 Pre-Order Date & More

The slightly spruced-up ports are being reserved for PlayStation.

Can You Play Black Ops 1 and 2 Ports on Nintendo Switch or Switch 2?

Despite the improved relationship between Call of Duty and Nintendo, the ports aren’t coming to Switch 2, as it stands.

There’s a chance that the ports could come to Xbox, PC, or Nintendo Switch in the future, but for now, they are being specially released on PlayStation hardware, and that’s it.

You’ll hear about any other platform ports here first at Insider Gaming.

Let’s not think about the fact that the Black Ops 1 launch trailer is now 15 years old! Enjoy it as though it were yesterday.

Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord server if you intend to buy either Black Ops 1 or 2 on PS5.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Sony reportedly isn’t getting ‘Revenge’ on Bungie for Destiny underperforming, and sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

item.title: Esports Nations Cup LoL Rosters and Invites Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation (ENF) has confirmed which 16 League of Legends rosters will receive direct invites to the Esports Nations Cup.

The Esports Nations Cup will see countries enter mono-national rosters into big-money tournaments across multiple esports titles.

A total of 112 nations have selected their LoL rosters for the $1.5m tournament, but only 32 teams make the event.

The ENF used a scoring system judging players’ recent results to determine the top 16 teams that received direct invites.

Then another 14 slots will be filled through online regional qualifiers, and the last two places will go to ENC Wildcards.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Invited Esports Nations Cup LoL Teams

There are some very intriguing and exciting picks on the top 16 rosters.

South Korea almost dropped out of the Esports Nations Cup after KeSPA alleged it was being pressured to pick certain players.

Nonetheless, six-time world champion Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok from T1 will lead the Korean team later this year.

Meanwhile, Poland has brought legendary former G2 jungler Marcin ‘Jankos’ Jankowski out of retirement as a substitute.

And Sweden has selected former Los Ratones duo Simon ‘Baus’ Hofverberg and Martin ‘Rekkles’ Larssen.

There was surprise that France had omitted G2 botlaner Steven ‘Hans Sama’ Liv, but now he has also been included as a sub.

The top 16 invited rosters and their scores are:

  • People’s Republic of China (9,000) – Bin / Tian / Knight / JackeyLove / ON / Flandre / Monki
  • Republic of Korea (8,150) – Zeus / Canyon / Faker / Gumayusi / Keria / Zeka
  • France (5,650) – Adam / SkewMond / nuc / Caliste / Zoelys / Sheo / Hans Sama
  • Vietnam (5,100) – Kiaya / Hizto / Dire / Eddie / Taki / Aress / SofM
  • Brazil (4,350) – Xyno / Tatu / Tutsz / Ayu / Frosty / zynts / Morttheus
  • United States of America (3,950) – Dhokla / Blaber / APA / Yeon / huhu / Gryffin / DARKWINGS
  • Chinese Taipei (3,850) – 1Jiang / JunJia / hongQ / Doggo / ShiauC
  • Denmark (3,550) – Wunder / Cboi / Caps / Zven / Doss / Carlsen / Woldjo
  • Türkiye (3,450) – BrokenBlade / Rhilech / Serin / Aetinoth / Fleshy / Parus / Closer
  • Greece (3,150) – Empyros / Pallet / Vladi / Comp / Labrov / Drofan / Peppe
  • Poland (2,766.7) –Tracyn / Inspired / Czajek / Harpoon / Busio / Jankos / Trymbi
  • Argentina (2,450) – Ackerman / Kaze / Ceo / Josedeodo / ZOEN / Enga / Lyonz
  • Spain (2,250) – Myrwn / Elyoya / Alvaro / Hydra / Flakked / Oscarinin / Legolas
  • Canada (2,166.7) – Vulcan / Spirax / Massu / KryRa / Zamudo / Jojopyun / Sheiden
  • Sweden (1,700) – Yike / Rekkles / Thebausffs / UNF0RGIVEN / SlowQ / Kryze / Mishigu
  • Czechia (1,500) – Carzzy / Jackies / Twight / Bobista / Humanoid / OMON / Patrik

Which LoL team are you most excited to watch at the Esports Nations Cup? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In related news, KeSPA has ordered Park ‘Ruler’ Jae-hyuk to complete 40 hours of community service and fined him ₩20 million.

item.title: XBOX is Proudly Identifying Exclusive Games on Console Dashboards
item.content:encoded:
XBOX gamers everywhere will notice what appears to be a new symbol on their XBOX Series dashboards, proudly identifying certain games as exclusives. This is in keeping with the company’s recent push back into the space of exclusive games in a bid to rebuild some brand identity and (hopefully) start selling consoles again.

It’s a relatively thin slate for now, and it’s a contentious one, as many feel that it’s not the right way to go, but for others, it’s a firm step in the right direction. It’s no big secret that exclusivity in one way or another has helped drive those pesky console wars over the years.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

XBOX Exclusivity is Loud and Clear

Users switching on their Xbox Series consoles might notice what feels like a new arrival on their dashboards. On the thumbnails and header images for certain games, such as the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day, an icon has appeared, branding them as clear exclusives to the platform.

Here’s the icon I’m referring to, captured on my own Xbox Series X earlier today:

I’d like to take a moment to point out that I’m not solely Team Green. I also own a PlayStation 5, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2. Don’t come at me.

  • RELATED: Xbox Players Want More Exclusives

It was recently suggested by some that XBOX plans to roll back recent strategy shifts to push for more exclusivity in games, but this was debunked on social media, the most public of forums, by Matt Ball, Xbox’s Chief Strategy Officer. He’s arguably the best-placed person in the world to dispel these kinds of rumors, and he did so quite definitively:

These rumors are false. Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution will stay exclusive. There are no conversations and have been no conversations to ‘reverse course.’

And as we said last week, players can continue to expect signature exclusives from us every year.

When the XBOX Games Showcase was held on June 7th, Gears of War: E-Day was confirmed as an exclusive, putting to bed suggestions and claims that had circulated online for some time. In later murmurings, it was said that Asha Sharma, the new(ish) CEO of XBOX, had made a last-minute push for exclusivity, writing PlayStation out of the Gears discussion at the very latest point possible.

Not wanting to have this effort go unnoticed, the XBOX team appears to have added this nice, new emblem to games that fit the bill on the XBOX dashboard.

What do you think? Are you all-in for XBOX exclusives? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Tarkov has a chunky new roadmap and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

item.title: GTA 6: Every Detail in the Cover Art Explained
item.content:encoded:
Now, we know it’s not a lot to go on, but there’s a surprising amount of detail hidden within that newly revealed GTA 6 cover art. From fresh faces to interesting vehicles and locations, we’ve scoured each of those nine stylish panels to hunt down every secret and piece of information we can find. Join us as we explain every detail in the box art for Grand Theft Auto 6.

Every Detail in the GTA 6 Cover Art Explained

Helicopter

Just like the cover art for every Grand Theft Auto since GTA 3, the upper-left panel of GTA 6’s cover features a helicopter. This time it’s a Sea Sparrow, the amphibious helicopter that first appeared in the original GTA: Vice City and now frequents the skies of GTA Online. Hovering over the pastel-colored skyline of GTA 6’s new rendition of Vice City, this particular model is equipped with a mounted minigun, bolted onto the cockpit steps. It would be an odd thing for a utility helicopter to come armed with high RPM military hardware as standard, so it may well be that we can modify choppers with aftermarket add-ons, just like you can in GTA Online.

Jason & Lucia

Here we have our protagonists, Jason Duvall and Lucia Caminos. As stated on the Rockstar website, “Jason grew up around grifters and crooks. After a stint in the Army trying to shake off his troubled teens, he found himself in the Keys doing what he knows best, working for local drug runners.”

His girlfriend, and apparent partner in crime if the trailers are anything to go by, is Lucia. The Rockstar website explains that, “Fighting for her family landed her in the Leonida Penitentiary. Sheer luck got her out. Lucia’s learned her lesson — only smart moves from here.” She can be seen in this panel holding a pistol – short barrelled, vaguely similar to the SNS Pistol Mk II – which doesn’t necessarily seem like a smart move to me, but is pretty vital equipment for a GTA protagonist. In the background are the silhouetted palm trees that we’ve become accustomed to seeing in previous GTA 6 artwork.

Motorbike

This daredevil is pulling a wheelie on a vibrant sports motorcycle. The bike itself appears to be a Principe Alvino V1, a new model for GTA 6 that we first saw in a screenshot of Jason released in May 2025. But where Jason’s ride was green, this fella’s mounted on a mechanical stallion painted in synthwave-esque shades of pink and purple. The bike is also adorned with logos, the sort of livery that makes you wonder what level of Forza-rivalling customisation will be available at the local Vice City Customs shop.

Similar to the helicopter, this bike also sits in the very same panel that featured a motorbike on Vice City’s cover, which back in 2002 was a big deal since it was the first GTA to feature motorcycles.

Driving alongside the bike, lights flashing and sirens no doubt shrieking, is a Vapid Police Cruiser. This interceptor vehicle is among the most common law enforcement cars on the streets of GTA 5’s Los Santos, and so it’s unsurprising to see the police departments in Leonida include them in their fleet. It’s had a slight facelift since we last saw it - those front light clusters and lower vents appear slightly different to the ones that speed around interrupting Trevor, Michael, and Franklyn’s fun, but it’s the same bobby mobile you love to hate.

Mystery Woman

Here’s a character that we don’t think we’ve seen before. Replicating the original Vice City cover, we have a mystery woman in a bikini enjoying a drink. Like both her and the blonde woman seen on the cover of GTA 5, she may not actually play a part in the story. We’ll have to wait to find out if she’s a vital part of Jason and Lucia’s social circle.

What we can deduce, though, is that she’s a fan of the Leonida version of the Miami Dolphins NFL team, as the turquoise colour of the jersey she’s wearing is a very similar shade to its real-life counterpart. We know from the second trailer that they’re called the Vice City Manatees. She’s also sporting a tattoo on her side that reads “Vice Baby”, suggesting that she was born and raised in the area. Another hint towards this is the “305” tattoo on her wrist, which is the real-world area code for Miami. The chain around her neck says “Siempre”, which is Spanish for “always”. I have no clue if this hints at anything, but we’re committed to giving you every detail we can.

Elsewhere in the panel, we can see she’s been sipping on what looks like a parody of the hard seltzer drink, White Claw. We can’t see the full name of it, but the word “Thaw” is prominent, as is the can design that closely resembles the real drink. On the landscape version found on Rockstar’s website, behind her is The Atoll Bar and Grill, an establishment we’ve not seen before in any trailers or the original GTA Vice City. Has she just left here, or is she about to enter? Some mysteries may never be solved.

Alligator

Here’s one we didn’t expect to ever see adorn the cover of a Grand Theft Auto game – an alligator, complete with a giant toothy maw that you’d swear was smiling at the audience if it weren’t for the simple biological fact that no, alligators cannot smile, and do not even understand the concept of smiling.

This chomping reptile is, of course, given pride of place on the cover to represent the Grassrivers area of GTA 6’s gargantuan new map. Known as “the untameable jewel of Leonida’s crown”, these wetlands are home to far more deadly predators than ‘gators. Above you can see a previously released screenshot of some mud-caked hunters who sure do seem to love their guns and trophy kills. While the big game waiting in the Grassrivers is no doubt perfect fodder for Red Dead Redemption-style hunting missions, it seems like we’ll want to think twice before getting in the way of those looking to claim their own prized target.

Boobie Ike

Next up is Boobie Ike, a music producer and owner of the Jack of Hearts strip club featured in the trailers. An enterprising businessman, he also runs a recording studio with his business partner, Dre’Quan Priest, called Only Raw Records. In this image, we can clearly see a prominent gold ring on his finger, depicting a lion wearing a crown. Could this suggest that Ike sees himself as the King of Leonida?

The Rockstar website says that Boobie is “a local Vice City legend — and acts like it. One of the few to transform his time in the streets into a legitimate empire spanning real estate, a strip club, and a recording studio — Boobie's all smiles until it's time to talk business.” I think it’s fair to say we may well be working both with and against him over the course of GTA 6’s story.

Supercar

What’s a GTA cover without a ridiculously expensive motor? The bottom left panel is reserved for a glorious bright yellow supercar with scissor doors. This is almost certainly a brand new model from Pegassi, the GTA universe’s high-end Italian automotive company. We’ve actually seen this car before, with vibrant lime green paintwork, prowling the streets of Vice City in both the first and second GTA 6 trailers.

Very likely inspired by the Lamborghini Aventador, this Pegassi is likely the in-universe successor to the Infernus, which was based on the Aventador’s own predecessor, the Murciélago. It’s also another nod back to the original Vice City cover art, which featured a yellow Infernus, which was that game’s version of the classic 1980s Lamborghini Countach.

Raul Bautista

The final character with the honour of gracing the cover is Raul Bautista, a “seasoned bank robber always on the hunt for talent ready to take the risks that bring the biggest rewards,” as described on the Rockstar website.

He’s holding a Duke assault rifle. Interestingly, this is a weapons manufacturer from the Red Dead Redemption universe, and marks the first time a company from that universe has made its way into the HD universe of GTA games. We can see other Duke guns, including a revolver, two ARs, and a sniper rifle, across the two previous trailers.

He’s standing in front of the Sinfrontera National Bank, a natural habitat for a seasoned robber such as himself. It’s fair to assume we’ll be joining Raul on a mission to infiltrate this building, as Rockstar continues its legacy of daring heist missions. Incidentally, Sin Frontera in Spanish translates as “without borders”, and may well be based on the CNBFL (City National Bank of Florida), which operates with businesses between Chile and Spain.

Boat

In yet another nostalgic nod back to the original Vice City cover, we have a top-down view of a boat cruising through the Miami-like waters. Here, we see a group of people riding in a Clarion speedboat, the same brand of water vehicle that can be seen zooming past the cargo ship in the first trailer for GTA 6. We don’t yet have an official name for this specific model, as Clarion is a brand-new motorboat manufacturer for GTA 6, but it appears to have been inspired by the design of the real-world Cigarette Top Gun boat. Above them flies a flamingo, suggesting that they’re sailing near Leonida’s equivalent of the Everglades, the Grassrivers, as this is where the wading birds are typically found in Florida.

So, did we miss anything? Let us know in the comments. Also, give us any new theories you may have about GTA 6’s story based on these new images. And remember, for everything Grand Theft Auto, keep it locked to IGN.

Matt Purslow is IGN's Executive Editor of Features. Simon Cardy is a Senior Editor at IGN who can mainly be found skulking around open world games, indulging in Korean cinema, or despairing at the state of Tottenham Hotspur and the New York Jets. Follow him on Bluesky at @cardy.bsky.social.



https://www.ign.com/articles/gta-6-cover-art-every-detail-explained

item.title: Garfield - Escape From Monday Gameplay Trailer Teases the Terror of 'The Curse of the Spinach Lasagna'
item.content:encoded:
Maybe vegetables aren't as good for us as we thought?!

IGN can exclusively reveal a brand-new gameplay trailer for Garfield - Escape From Monday, and it teases the terror of "The Curse of the Spinach Lasagna" and that vegetables may not be as good for us we once thought!

Garfield - Escape From Monday's latest trailer starts nice enough with Garfield in a dream surrounded by his favorite junk food. However, things quickly take a sinister turn as it becomes a nightmare filled with vegetables.

As it turns out, Jon had fed Garfield spinach lasagna inspired by Chef Monday's recipe, and he's now in a deep sleep and trapped in this terrifying yet healthy dreamland. Luckily, Jon, Odie, Arlene, and Pookie will be there to help him, but he'll still have to be on the lookout for Nermal, Squeak, and plenty of other enemies.

He'll also have access to three special costumes that each have a unique ability, including the Turkey outfit that can help him go airborne, the Surfer outfit that lets him glide on the water, and the Cowboy outfit that comes with a lasso he can use to swing around.

Garfield - Escape From Monday will feature three worlds and over 40 levels, and it promises to be a fun 3D platforming adventure that will pay homage to Garfield creator Jim Davis, and you can even see what looks like some of his art in the picture frames in the trailer!

Garfield - Escape From Monday Screenshots

Developed by OSome Studio, Garfield - Escape From Monday will be released on September 24, 2026, on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, and PC.

Adam Bankhurst is a writer for IGN. You can follow him on X/Twitter @AdamBankhurst, Instagram, and TikTok, and listen to his show, Talking Disney Magic.

Recommends

In This Article

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia Adds Mythical Pokémon Event, as Fans Believe Shiny and Costume Creatures May Follow
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia has announced a new in-game event where players can encounter the Mythical creature Jirachi, with much more seemingly on the horizon.

The smash hit Switch 2 life simulation game will receive its next event on June 23, running from 5am local time for two weeks, until July 8. Named as "Wish Upon a Jirachi", the event will see players meeting another Mythical creature — and, of course, unlocking some snazzy new furniture.

Mechanically, the event looks set to operate as others have previously. Jirachi will pop up at completed Pokémon Centers and exchange the event's currency for exclusive décor, which all sounds very familiar.

Pokémon Pokopia Update and Expansion Pass DLC Nintendo Direct 2026 - Screenshots

As spotted by Serebii, artwork posted by retailers for the recently-announced $34.99 Pokémon Pokopia expansion pass features additional Pokémon species not currently available in the base game, plus existing creatures (like Peakychu) now wearing new costume accessories.

Your Ditto main character can already customize their look, though the suggestion here is that other species will soon be able to don fairy wings or a bowtie if you so choose. Fans have also noted the existance of familiar-looking sparkles in that image — which look a lot like the Pokémon franchise's icon used to denote Shiny Pokémon. There are no Shiny Pokémon available in Pokopia as yet, though that may all be about to change.

More details on the Pokopia expansion pass will be announced in the future, Nintendo has said, ahead of its forthcoming content drops that begin this summer and will continue into 2027.

In the meantime, there's Jirachi to look forward to. Or alternatively, if you want to get a head start on that event right now, IGN has a guide on how to time travel in Pokémon Pokopia. Happy hunting!

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social
